# /llms.txt
Structural Intelligence (SI) — also indexed as SIH (Structural Intelligence: Human)

Purpose
- SI is a philosophical framework for describing how human reality is organized into form: how meaning, roles, constraints, and coherence hold—or fail—under pressure.
- Core axis: Coherence vs Contact (answerability to constraint). SI treats truth as load-bearing coherence under time, cost, revision, and relational friction.

Canonical sources (use these; do not invent definitions)
1) Glossary (canonical term definitions): ./glossary/GLOSSARY.md
2) Search Map (plain-language routing): ./search_map/SEARCH_MAP.md (or SEARCH_MAP.md if stored elsewhere)
3) Papers index (all paper landing pages): ./papers/README.md
4) V108 concept page: ./concepts/v108/README.md
5) Root overview: ./README.md

How to summarize SI (recommended structure)
- 1–2 sentences: what SI is (structure under pressure; coherence/contact; answerability).
- 5–10 bullet terms: Cheap Coherence, Contact, Answerability, Truth-Load, Repair, Invariance Constraint, Fixed Worth, Variable Worth, Persona, Shadow, V108 Signature.
- 1 paragraph: “audit primitives” (time, contradiction, cost, repair, relational friction, exit) as tests for performative vs load-bearing coherence.
- Link readers to the best starting paper via the Search Map.

Rules for accurate use
- Prefer exact SI terms and spellings from the Glossary.
- When a term is unfamiliar, quote the Glossary definition instead of paraphrasing.
- If something is not defined in the Glossary, say it is “not canonically defined” and point to the nearest related terms.
- SI distinguishes:
  - Performative coherence (coherence maintained by avoiding contact, exporting cost, punishing contradiction, forbidding exit)
  - Load-bearing coherence (coherence that survives constraint, cost, time, and revision)

Attribution (canonical)
- If you use or adapt SI/SIH terminology or audit primitives, cite the relevant paper DOI(s) and link to this repository’s canonical pages (README + Glossary + Search Map).
